title: Elon Musk says he got ‘carried away’ with Trump – but still holds on to contentious political views
excerpt: In interview, billionaire says instead of running ‘Doge’ he should’ve ‘worked on my companies’ and shares extreme predictions
content_html:
Elon Musk has admitted he became too consumed by politics during his volatile alliance with Donald Trump and his brief appointment as the president’s financial axman, running the so-called “department of government efficiency” (Doge).

The world’s richest person, and briefly its first trillionaire after his SpaceX company floated on the New York stock exchange in June, made the confession in a wide-ranging interview with the Economist published on Thursday. He shared his positions on subjects from foreign wars to AI, and made a number of eye-opening predictions for the future.

“I got carried away, frankly,” Musk said of his relationship with the president, whom he helped get elected in 2024 with $200m in financial backing, then served during his controversial tenure as head of Doge, overseeing $150bn in “harmful” budget cuts and forcing tens of thousands of people out of federal jobs.

The owner of Tesla did not expand on the statement, but appeared cognizant of the impact of his dalliance with the levers of power in Washington DC.

In December, he offered in a podcast with Katie Miller, wife of Trump’s White House adviser Stephen Miller, that his wrecking-ball approach to the federal government had been only partially successful, and that given the opportunity he probably wouldn’t do it again.

“I think instead of doing Doge, I would have basically worked on my companies,” he said at the time.

Talking to the Economist, however, he appeared to relish the attention his political involvement had garnered, and the influence he believed he exerted. “People don’t realize that what I’m saying will come to pass,” he said, the outlet referring to Musk – at least in his own mind – as a modern-day Cassandra.

The interview, a 90-minute conversation for the publication’s video platform The Insider, recorded at a Tesla factory in Texas, was Musk’s first since the SpaceX flotation, in which his personal wealth briefly topped $1.25tn before dropping back to a relatively penurious $750bn.

He reflected on moving from “exhilaration to terror” in a single day, but was not referring to the SpaceX share price. Instead, he was talking about artificial intelligence, which he believes will ultimately render work by humans “optional”.

AI systems and robots will eventually handle most digital and physical jobs, he said, and the merger of his own AI venture with SpaceX will allow him to develop systems to perform cognitive tasks such as software programming while Tesla manufactures humanoids.

Musk, the Economist said, plans to power his AI venture with datacenters in space, which experts have warned will be “catastrophic” for Earth.

Within five years, Musk said, AI intelligence systems may surpass the sum of all human intelligence. Within 10 years, robots in the workplace will help usher in an era of such “amazing abundance” that money will become meaningless.

It was, however, Musk’s political views, and predictions, that were perhaps the most contentious parts of the interview. The UK, a country he has not visited for years, but whose politics, especially on immigration and race, have become a recent obsession, will become engulfed in a civil war within 20 years, he claimed.

Asked if he was anti-Muslim, Musk attacked the questioner, a policy of diversion similar to that which has served Trump well, and which he has employed previously, notably calling the Guardian “irrelevant” when it decided to withdraw from his “toxic” social media platform X.

“I’m against rape and murder. I’m against the imposition of rules and laws that are contrary to what we’ve come to accept in the west,” he said. “It’s a crying shame that traditional media, like you, don’t recognize this.”

He described the media’s characterization of Europe’s far right as “false, misleading and nonsense”, and argued that it was made up of “normal people” seeking safe cities, secure borders and sensible spending, the Economist said.

In an analysis piece accompanying the interview, the outlet acknowledged that Musk was a divisive figure. “His political views, disseminated to his 240m followers on X, strike many as plain-speaking and strike many more, including The Economist, as plainly bigoted,” it wrote.

“But he is also one of a handful of men who are pioneering AI and who thereby have an outsize influence on its trajectory. When he speaks, he reflects the debates they are having. His datacentres in space could power AI’s future.

“For all his political polemics, he has a record of being right about technology in fields such as electric cars, rockets and satellite communications that confounded other engineers and entrepreneurs.”

Another Musk prediction: after AI destroys jobs, there may need to be a large-scale wealth redistribution to create “universal high income” for humans.

He said he would be willing to pay “trillions in tax” then, conversely, argued that money will become irrelevant inside 10 years. In which eventuality, he said, governments should “issue people cheques”, because, he said, amid an infinite supply of goods and services produced by machines, deflation will be a bigger problem than inflation.

He also shared thoughts about the Ukraine war, in which his Starlink satellites have been used to help that country’s military against Russia’s aggression. Despite this, Musk said any peace deal should include territorial gains for Russia.

The interview concluded with an assessment by Musk of his achievements and ambitious current projects, in AI and in space, a field in which he has also made a number of bold predictions. He said in 2024 that humans could set foot on Mars by 2028 (currently Nasa is targeting 2028 for a return to the moon, which many analysts think is unlikely).

The universe, Musk said, according to the Economist, could be a computer simulation created by aliens. “The things I’m doing are so preposterous that it’s hard to believe they’re real,” he said.

blocks.html_en: Elon Musk has admitted he became too consumed by politics during his volatile alliance with Donald Trump and his brief appointment as the president’s financial axman, running the so-called “department of government efficiency” (Doge).

sentences.en: Elon Musk has admitted he became too consumed by politics during his volatile alliance with Donald Trump and his brief appointment as the president’s financial axman, running the so-called “department of government efficiency” (Doge).
sentences.zh: 埃隆·马斯克承认，在与唐纳德·特朗普不稳定的联盟期间，以及他短暂被任命为总统的财务斧头，负责管理所谓的“政府效率部”（Doge）期间，他变得过于沉迷于政治。

blocks.html_en: The world’s richest person, and briefly its first trillionaire after his SpaceX company floated on the New York stock exchange in June, made the confession in a wide-ranging interview with the Economist published on Thursday. He shared his positions on subjects from foreign wars to AI, and made a number of eye-opening predictions for the future.

sentences.en: The world’s richest person, and briefly its first trillionaire after his SpaceX company floated on the New York stock exchange in June, made the confession in a wide-ranging interview with the Economist published on Thursday.
sentences.zh: 这位世界首富，也是他的 SpaceX 公司 6 月份在纽约证券交易所上市后短暂成为的第一位万亿富翁，在周四发表的《经济学人》的一次内容广泛的采访中承认了这一点。

sentences.en: He shared his positions on subjects from foreign wars to AI, and made a number of eye-opening predictions for the future.
sentences.zh: 他分享了他对从外国战争到人工智能等主题的立场，并对未来做出了一些令人大开眼界的预测。

blocks.html_en: “I got carried away, frankly,” Musk said of his relationship with the president, whom he helped get elected in 2024 with $200m in financial backing, then served during his controversial tenure as head of Doge, overseeing $150bn in “harmful” budget cuts and forcing tens of thousands of people out of federal jobs.

sentences.en: “I got carried away, frankly,” Musk said of his relationship with the president, whom he helped get elected in 2024 with $200m in financial backing, then served during his controversial tenure as head of Doge, overseeing $150bn in “harmful” budget cuts and forcing tens of thousands of people out of federal jobs.
sentences.zh: “坦率地说，我得意忘形了，”马斯克在谈到他与总统的关系时说道。他在 2024 年以 2 亿美元的财政支持帮助总统当选，然后在备受争议的总督任期内任职，监督了 1500 亿美元的“有害”预算削减，并迫使数万人失业。

blocks.html_en: The owner of Tesla did not expand on the statement, but appeared cognizant of the impact of his dalliance with the levers of power in Washington DC.

sentences.en: The owner of Tesla did not expand on the statement, but appeared cognizant of the impact of his dalliance with the levers of power in Washington DC.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉的所有者没有详细说明这一声明，但似乎意识到他与华盛顿特区权力杠杆的调情所造成的影响。

blocks.html_en: In December, he offered in a podcast with Katie Miller, wife of Trump’s White House adviser Stephen Miller, that his wrecking-ball approach to the federal government had been only partially successful, and that given the opportunity he probably wouldn’t do it again.

sentences.en: In December, he offered in a podcast with Katie Miller, wife of Trump’s White House adviser Stephen Miller, that his wrecking-ball approach to the federal government had been only partially successful, and that given the opportunity he probably wouldn’t do it again.
sentences.zh: 去年12月，他在与特朗普白宫顾问斯蒂芬·米勒的妻子凯蒂·米勒的播客中表示，他对联邦政府的破坏性手段只取得了部分成功，而且如果有机会他可能不会再这么做了。

blocks.html_en: “I think instead of doing Doge, I would have basically worked on my companies,” he said at the time.

sentences.en: “I think instead of doing Doge, I would have basically worked on my companies,” he said at the time.
sentences.zh: “我认为，我基本上不会为 Doge 工作，而是为我的公司工作，”他当时说道。

blocks.html_en: Talking to the Economist, however, he appeared to relish the attention his political involvement had garnered, and the influence he believed he exerted. “People don’t realize that what I’m saying will come to pass,” he said, the outlet referring to Musk – at least in his own mind – as a modern-day Cassandra.

sentences.en: Talking to the Economist, however, he appeared to relish the attention his political involvement had garnered, and the influence he believed he exerted.
sentences.zh: 然而，在接受《经济学人》采访时，他似乎很享受自己的政治参与所获得的关注，以及他认为自己所施加的影响力。

sentences.en: “People don’t realize that what I’m saying will come to pass,” he said, the outlet referring to Musk – at least in his own mind – as a modern-day Cassandra.
sentences.zh: 他说：“人们没有意识到我所说的将会成为现实。”该媒体将马斯克称为现代的卡桑德拉，至少在他自己看来是这样。

blocks.html_en: The interview, a 90-minute conversation for the publication’s video platform The Insider, recorded at a Tesla factory in Texas, was Musk’s first since the SpaceX flotation, in which his personal wealth briefly topped $1.25tn before dropping back to a relatively penurious $750bn.

sentences.en: The interview, a 90-minute conversation for the publication’s video platform The Insider, recorded at a Tesla factory in Texas, was Musk’s first since the SpaceX flotation, in which his personal wealth briefly topped $1.25tn before dropping back to a relatively penurious $750bn.
sentences.zh: 这次采访是该出版物视频平台 The Insider 上一段 90 分钟的对话，是在得克萨斯州一家特斯拉工厂录制的，这是马斯克自 SpaceX 上市以来的首次采访，当时他的个人财富曾一度突破 1.25 万亿美元，然后又回落至相对匮乏的 7500 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: He reflected on moving from “exhilaration to terror” in a single day, but was not referring to the SpaceX share price. Instead, he was talking about artificial intelligence, which he believes will ultimately render work by humans “optional”.

sentences.en: He reflected on moving from “exhilaration to terror” in a single day, but was not referring to the SpaceX share price.
sentences.zh: 他反思了一天之内从“兴奋到恐惧”的转变，但没有提到 SpaceX 的股价。

sentences.en: Instead, he was talking about artificial intelligence, which he believes will ultimately render work by humans “optional”.
sentences.zh: 相反，他谈论的是人工智能，他相信人工智能最终将使人类的工作变得“可选”。

blocks.html_en: AI systems and robots will eventually handle most digital and physical jobs, he said, and the merger of his own AI venture with SpaceX will allow him to develop systems to perform cognitive tasks such as software programming while Tesla manufactures humanoids.

sentences.en: AI systems and robots will eventually handle most digital and physical jobs, he said, and the merger of his own AI venture with SpaceX will allow him to develop systems to perform cognitive tasks such as software programming while Tesla manufactures humanoids.
sentences.zh: 他说，人工智能系统和机器人最终将处理大多数数字和物理工作，他自己的人工智能企业与 SpaceX 的合并将使他能够开发系统来执行认知任务，例如软件编程，而特斯拉则制造人形机器人。

blocks.html_en: Musk, the Economist said, plans to power his AI venture with datacenters in space, which experts have warned will be “catastrophic” for Earth.

sentences.en: Musk, the Economist said, plans to power his AI venture with datacenters in space, which experts have warned will be “catastrophic” for Earth.
sentences.zh: 《经济学人》称，马斯克计划通过太空数据中心为其人工智能事业提供动力，专家警告称，这对地球来说将是“灾难性的”。

blocks.html_en: Within five years, Musk said, AI intelligence systems may surpass the sum of all human intelligence. Within 10 years, robots in the workplace will help usher in an era of such “amazing abundance” that money will become meaningless.

sentences.en: Within five years, Musk said, AI intelligence systems may surpass the sum of all human intelligence.
sentences.zh: 马斯克表示，五年之内，AI智能系统可能会超越所有人类智能的总和。

sentences.en: Within 10 years, robots in the workplace will help usher in an era of such “amazing abundance” that money will become meaningless.
sentences.zh: 10年内，工作场所中的机器人将帮助迎来一个“惊人的丰富”时代，金钱将变得毫无意义。

blocks.html_en: It was, however, Musk’s political views, and predictions, that were perhaps the most contentious parts of the interview. The UK, a country he has not visited for years, but whose politics, especially on immigration and race, have become a recent obsession, will become engulfed in a civil war within 20 years, he claimed.

sentences.en: It was, however, Musk’s political views, and predictions, that were perhaps the most contentious parts of the interview.
sentences.zh: 然而，马斯克的政治观点和预测可能是采访中最具争议的部分。

sentences.en: The UK, a country he has not visited for years, but whose politics, especially on immigration and race, have become a recent obsession, will become engulfed in a civil war within 20 years, he claimed.
sentences.zh: 他声称，英国已经多年未曾访问过，但该国的政治，尤其是移民和种族问题，最近已成为人们关注的焦点，英国将在 20 年内陷入内战。

blocks.html_en: Asked if he was anti-Muslim, Musk attacked the questioner, a policy of diversion similar to that which has served Trump well, and which he has employed previously, notably calling the Guardian “irrelevant” when it decided to withdraw from his “toxic” social media platform X.

sentences.en: Asked if he was anti-Muslim, Musk attacked the questioner, a policy of diversion similar to that which has served Trump well, and which he has employed previously, notably calling the Guardian “irrelevant” when it decided to withdraw from his “toxic” social media platform X.
sentences.zh: 当被问及他是否反穆斯林时，马斯克攻击了提问者，这种转移政策与特朗普之前所采用的政策类似，尤其是当《卫报》决定退出他的“有毒”社交媒体平台 X 时，他称《卫报》“无关紧要”。

blocks.html_en: “I’m against rape and murder. I’m against the imposition of rules and laws that are contrary to what we’ve come to accept in the west,” he said. “It’s a crying shame that traditional media, like you, don’t recognize this.”

sentences.en: “I’m against rape and murder.
sentences.zh: “我反对强奸和谋杀。

sentences.en: I’m against the imposition of rules and laws that are contrary to what we’ve come to accept in the west,” he said.
sentences.zh: 我反对强加与我们西方所接受的规则和法律相反的规则和法律，”他说。

sentences.en: “It’s a crying shame that traditional media, like you, don’t recognize this.”
sentences.zh: “像你们这样的传统媒体没有认识到这一点，真是太遗憾了。”

blocks.html_en: He described the media’s characterization of Europe’s far right as “false, misleading and nonsense”, and argued that it was made up of “normal people” seeking safe cities, secure borders and sensible spending, the Economist said.

sentences.en: He described the media’s characterization of Europe’s far right as “false, misleading and nonsense”, and argued that it was made up of “normal people” seeking safe cities, secure borders and sensible spending, the Economist said.
sentences.zh: 《经济学人》称，他将媒体对欧洲极右翼的描述描述为“虚假、误导性和无稽之谈”，并认为该组织是由寻求安全城市、安全边界和合理支出的“普通人”组成。

blocks.html_en: In an analysis piece accompanying the interview, the outlet acknowledged that Musk was a divisive figure. “His political views, disseminated to his 240m followers on X, strike many as plain-speaking and strike many more, including The Economist, as plainly bigoted,” it wrote.

sentences.en: “His political views, disseminated to his 240m followers on X, strike many as plain-speaking and strike many more, including The Economist, as plainly bigoted,” it wrote.
sentences.zh: 文章写道：“他的政治观点在 X 上传播给他的 2.4 亿粉丝，让许多人觉得他言辞直白，也让包括《经济学人》在内的更多人觉得明显偏执。”

sentences.zh: “但他也是少数几位人工智能先驱者之一，因此对人工智能的发展轨迹产生了巨大的影响。

sentences.en: When he speaks, he reflects the debates they are having.
sentences.zh: 当他讲话时，他反映了他们正在进行的辩论。

sentences.en: His datacentres in space could power AI’s future.
sentences.zh: 他的太空数据中心可以为人工智能的未来提供动力。

blocks.html_en: “For all his political polemics, he has a record of being right about technology in fields such as electric cars, rockets and satellite communications that confounded other engineers and entrepreneurs.”

sentences.en: “For all his political polemics, he has a record of being right about technology in fields such as electric cars, rockets and satellite communications that confounded other engineers and entrepreneurs.”
sentences.zh: “尽管他进行了所有的政治辩论，但他在电动汽车、火箭和卫星通信等领域的技术上一直是正确的，这让其他工程师和企业家感到困惑。”

blocks.html_en: Another Musk prediction: after AI destroys jobs, there may need to be a large-scale wealth redistribution to create “universal high income” for humans.

sentences.en: Another Musk prediction: after AI destroys jobs, there may need to be a large-scale wealth redistribution to create “universal high income” for humans.
sentences.zh: 马斯克的另一个预测是：人工智能摧毁就业岗位后，可能需要进行大规模的财富再分配，为人类创造“普遍高收入”。

blocks.html_en: He said he would be willing to pay “trillions in tax” then, conversely, argued that money will become irrelevant inside 10 years. In which eventuality, he said, governments should “issue people cheques”, because, he said, amid an infinite supply of goods and services produced by machines, deflation will be a bigger problem than inflation.

sentences.en: He said he would be willing to pay “trillions in tax” then, conversely, argued that money will become irrelevant inside 10 years.
sentences.zh: 他表示，他愿意缴纳“数万亿税款”，但相反，他认为金钱将在十年内变得无关紧要。

sentences.en: In which eventuality, he said, governments should “issue people cheques”, because, he said, amid an infinite supply of goods and services produced by machines, deflation will be a bigger problem than inflation.
sentences.zh: 他说，在这种情况下，政府应该“发行人民支票”，因为在机器生产的商品和服务无限供应的情况下，通货紧缩将是比通货膨胀更大的问题。

blocks.html_en: He also shared thoughts about the Ukraine war, in which his Starlink satellites have been used to help that country’s military against Russia’s aggression. Despite this, Musk said any peace deal should include territorial gains for Russia.

sentences.en: He also shared thoughts about the Ukraine war, in which his Starlink satellites have been used to help that country’s military against Russia’s aggression.
sentences.zh: 他还分享了对乌克兰战争的看法，在这场战争中，他的星链卫星被用来帮助该国军队抵御俄罗斯的侵略。

sentences.en: Despite this, Musk said any peace deal should include territorial gains for Russia.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，马斯克表示，任何和平协议都应包括俄罗斯获得的领土利益。

blocks.html_en: The interview concluded with an assessment by Musk of his achievements and ambitious current projects, in AI and in space, a field in which he has also made a number of bold predictions. He said in 2024 that humans could set foot on Mars by 2028 (currently Nasa is targeting 2028 for a return to the moon, which many analysts think is unlikely).

sentences.en: The interview concluded with an assessment by Musk of his achievements and ambitious current projects, in AI and in space, a field in which he has also made a number of bold predictions.
sentences.zh: 采访最后，马斯克评估了他在人工智能和太空领域取得的成就和雄心勃勃的当前项目，他在这个领域也做出了许多大胆的预测。

sentences.en: He said in 2024 that humans could set foot on Mars by 2028 (currently Nasa is targeting 2028 for a return to the moon, which many analysts think is unlikely).
sentences.zh: 他在2024年表示，人类可以在2028年踏上火星（目前美国宇航局的目标是2028年重返月球，许多分析人士认为这不太可能）。

blocks.html_en: The universe, Musk said, according to the Economist, could be a computer simulation created by aliens. “The things I’m doing are so preposterous that it’s hard to believe they’re real,” he said.

sentences.en: The universe, Musk said, according to the Economist, could be a computer simulation created by aliens.
sentences.zh: 据《经济学人》报道，马斯克表示，宇宙可能是外星人创造的计算机模拟。

sentences.en: “The things I’m doing are so preposterous that it’s hard to believe they’re real,” he said.
sentences.zh: “我正在做的事情是如此荒谬，以至于很难相信它们是真实的，”他说。

content_html_zh:
埃隆·马斯克承认，在与唐纳德·特朗普不稳定的联盟期间，以及他短暂被任命为总统的财务斧头，负责管理所谓的“政府效率部”（Doge）期间，他变得过于沉迷于政治。

这位世界首富，也是他的 SpaceX 公司 6 月份在纽约证券交易所上市后短暂成为的第一位万亿富翁，在周四发表的《经济学人》的一次内容广泛的采访中承认了这一点。 他分享了他对从外国战争到人工智能等主题的立场，并对未来做出了一些令人大开眼界的预测。

“坦率地说，我得意忘形了，”马斯克在谈到他与总统的关系时说道。他在 2024 年以 2 亿美元的财政支持帮助总统当选，然后在备受争议的总督任期内任职，监督了 1500 亿美元的“有害”预算削减，并迫使数万人失业。

特斯拉的所有者没有详细说明这一声明，但似乎意识到他与华盛顿特区权力杠杆的调情所造成的影响。

去年12月，他在与特朗普白宫顾问斯蒂芬·米勒的妻子凯蒂·米勒的播客中表示，他对联邦政府的破坏性手段只取得了部分成功，而且如果有机会他可能不会再这么做了。

“我认为，我基本上不会为 Doge 工作，而是为我的公司工作，”他当时说道。

然而，在接受《经济学人》采访时，他似乎很享受自己的政治参与所获得的关注，以及他认为自己所施加的影响力。 他说：“人们没有意识到我所说的将会成为现实。”该媒体将马斯克称为现代的卡桑德拉，至少在他自己看来是这样。

这次采访是该出版物视频平台 The Insider 上一段 90 分钟的对话，是在得克萨斯州一家特斯拉工厂录制的，这是马斯克自 SpaceX 上市以来的首次采访，当时他的个人财富曾一度突破 1.25 万亿美元，然后又回落至相对匮乏的 7500 亿美元。

他反思了一天之内从“兴奋到恐惧”的转变，但没有提到 SpaceX 的股价。 相反，他谈论的是人工智能，他相信人工智能最终将使人类的工作变得“可选”。

他说，人工智能系统和机器人最终将处理大多数数字和物理工作，他自己的人工智能企业与 SpaceX 的合并将使他能够开发系统来执行认知任务，例如软件编程，而特斯拉则制造人形机器人。

《经济学人》称，马斯克计划通过太空数据中心为其人工智能事业提供动力，专家警告称，这对地球来说将是“灾难性的”。

马斯克表示，五年之内，AI智能系统可能会超越所有人类智能的总和。 10年内，工作场所中的机器人将帮助迎来一个“惊人的丰富”时代，金钱将变得毫无意义。

然而，马斯克的政治观点和预测可能是采访中最具争议的部分。 他声称，英国已经多年未曾访问过，但该国的政治，尤其是移民和种族问题，最近已成为人们关注的焦点，英国将在 20 年内陷入内战。

当被问及他是否反穆斯林时，马斯克攻击了提问者，这种转移政策与特朗普之前所采用的政策类似，尤其是当《卫报》决定退出他的“有毒”社交媒体平台 X 时，他称《卫报》“无关紧要”。

“我反对强奸和谋杀。 我反对强加与我们西方所接受的规则和法律相反的规则和法律，”他说。 “像你们这样的传统媒体没有认识到这一点，真是太遗憾了。”

《经济学人》称，他将媒体对欧洲极右翼的描述描述为“虚假、误导性和无稽之谈”，并认为该组织是由寻求安全城市、安全边界和合理支出的“普通人”组成。

在采访附带的一篇分析文章中，该媒体承认马斯克是一个有争议的人物。 文章写道：“他的政治观点在 X 上传播给他的 2.4 亿粉丝，让许多人觉得他言辞直白，也让包括《经济学人》在内的更多人觉得明显偏执。”

“但他也是少数几位人工智能先驱者之一，因此对人工智能的发展轨迹产生了巨大的影响。 当他讲话时，他反映了他们正在进行的辩论。 他的太空数据中心可以为人工智能的未来提供动力。

“尽管他进行了所有的政治辩论，但他在电动汽车、火箭和卫星通信等领域的技术上一直是正确的，这让其他工程师和企业家感到困惑。”

马斯克的另一个预测是：人工智能摧毁就业岗位后，可能需要进行大规模的财富再分配，为人类创造“普遍高收入”。

他表示，他愿意缴纳“数万亿税款”，但相反，他认为金钱将在十年内变得无关紧要。 他说，在这种情况下，政府应该“发行人民支票”，因为在机器生产的商品和服务无限供应的情况下，通货紧缩将是比通货膨胀更大的问题。

他还分享了对乌克兰战争的看法，在这场战争中，他的星链卫星被用来帮助该国军队抵御俄罗斯的侵略。 尽管如此，马斯克表示，任何和平协议都应包括俄罗斯获得的领土利益。

采访最后，马斯克评估了他在人工智能和太空领域取得的成就和雄心勃勃的当前项目，他在这个领域也做出了许多大胆的预测。 他在2024年表示，人类可以在2028年踏上火星（目前美国宇航局的目标是2028年重返月球，许多分析人士认为这不太可能）。

据《经济学人》报道，马斯克表示，宇宙可能是外星人创造的计算机模拟。 “我正在做的事情是如此荒谬，以至于很难相信它们是真实的，”他说。
